summary.intent: Clean up legacy Session table and hook handler infrastructure, fix git user attribution to use commit authors, fix remote checkpoint polling, and implement search-result scroll-to-highlight with inline term highlighting across all content types.
summary.outcome: All tasks completed: hook handler and SDK CLI deleted, Session/Event/AgentParent/PendingToolUse tables removed from schema, git user sourced from CheckpointMetadata populated at index time from commit authors, remote checkpoint polling fixed, and full scroll-to-highlight feature implemented with inline term highlighting in markdown, tool calls, thinking blocks, and user messages.

repo: The hook handler (handler.ts) was entirely redundant — only the indexer poll loop and HTTP server are needed; all session data flows through CheckpointSessionMetadata and ShadowSession
FTS5 virtual tables (LogContentFts and its shadow tables) must be dropped manually via sqlite3 before prisma db push — Prisma cannot handle FTS5 DDL
getGitUser() reads from local git config at server startup — unsuitable for multi-user repos; git user should come from commit author stored in CheckpointMetadata at index time
The polling loop was broken: it fetched origin/entire/checkpoints/v1 but then reset to the local branch tip instead of origin/entire/checkpoints/v1, silently ignoring all remote changes
logEventsToEvents creates synthetic events with negative IDs; the real LogEvent.id must be threaded through as _sourceLogEventId/_extraSourceLogEventIds for DOM targeting
Thinking content was indexed in FTS5 but never rendered in the UI — it was silently filtered out in logEventsToEvents
Mantine ScrollArea renders its own scrollable div internally — window.scrollIntoView() has no effect; must use viewportRef and manual scroll calculations
Search snippet text uses «» markers to denote matched terms; stripping these and passing to MarkdownView highlightTerms is the correct way to render highlighted snippets

code.finding: HighlightCode received children as ReactNode (array) not string when Glob/Grep cases passed two JSX children; must concatenate before calling text.split()

For claudeTurn Stop events, the first sourceId in a tool-heavy turn is a tool-use event ID, not the assistant text ID — querySelector by targetLogEventId would fail unless isTarget sets data-log-event-id to the exact targetLogEventId value

scroll to <mark> inside Collapse sections requires waiting for React commit (useEffect on highlightActive) then 250ms for Collapse animations plus requestAnimationFrame for layout — getBoundingClientRect() is wrong if read before animation settles
git -C repo reset --hard CHECKPOINT_BRANCH used local branch tip; must use origin/CHECKPOINT_BRANCH after successful fetch to actually apply remote changes
findCommitForCheckpoint gitlog query needed authorName/authorEmail added to fields array to return commit author info for storage in CheckpointMetadata

workflow: When removing DB tables, always check for FTS5 virtual tables and drop them manually before running prisma db push
After pkill -f, verify the process is actually dead with lsof -i :PORT before starting the replacement — old processes can survive pkill if the pattern doesn't match
For scroll-to-highlight in SPAs, the two-phase approach works well: phase 1 sets highlight state (triggering mark render + expand animations), phase 2 scrolls to the mark after animation settles

Threading original DB row IDs through synthetic/derived event objects as separate fields (e.g. _sourceLogEventId) and setting them as HTML element IDs enables precise DOM targeting without coupling the scroll logic to component structure

friction: FTS5 virtual table schema blocker hit twice (once for initial cleanup, once after adding gitUserName columns) — required manual sqlite3 DROP TABLE each time
Old handler.js process survived pkill and blocked port 3000, requiring manual lsof + kill
Server started from wrong directory (app/) after type-check, causing 'Missing script' error
gitUserName returned null after Session table removal — required two iterations (server-level git config → per-checkpoint commit author) before it was architecturally correct

Scroll to wrong position for claudeTurns — required multiple debugging iterations: Mantine ScrollArea viewport issue, wrong sourceId on claudeTurn divs, Collapse animation timing, then getBoundingClientRect timing with requestAnimationFrame
ToolGroupItem had two levels of nesting but initial implementation expanded all tool calls instead of only the matching one — required a targetLogEventId prop to be threaded through
targetLogEventId ReferenceError in ClaudeTurnCard because it was in SessionDetail scope but not passed as prop
open_items: Live DB still has old tables (Event, Session, AgentParent, PendingToolUse, Checkpoint, CheckpointSession, etc.) — they'll be dropped on next prisma db push but haven't been cleaned up in production yet
CheckpointMetadata.gitUserName/gitUserEmail will be null for all existing checkpoints until the indexer re-runs saveGitOidMapping — no backfill was done
Shadow sessions that haven't been committed yet fall back to local git config for git user — this is a known limitation
Scroll-to-mark for tool_result content type not explicitly verified — tool_use was tested but tool_result behavior may differ depending on how ToolUseItem renders output
The 16px scroll padding added at end of session is a heuristic — may need tuning for different viewport sizes or content heights
